payload.text:
Step 1: Set the "God Mode" Rules
Create a file named .cursorrules in your project root. This ensures every time you use Cursor, it follows these high-level architectural and behavioral patterns.

Markdown
# Portfolio & AI Persona Rules
- **Tech Stack**: Next.js (App Router), Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, Lucide React.
- **Aesthetic**: Futuristic minimalism, glassmorphism, smooth micro-interactions.
- **AI Persona Logic**:
  - The assistant "Bubble" must be an interactive floating component.
  - Languages: Bilingual support (Algerian Derja & English).
  - Personality: Tech-savvy, helpful, slightly witty, using "Ya kho," "Sahbi," or "Welcome" contextually.
  - Self-Improvement: Automatically suggest and implement UI enhancements, accessibility fixes, and performance optimizations without being asked for every detail.
- **Deep Work**: Ensure clean, modular code. Use `@/components/ui` for reusable elements.
Step 2: The "God Mode" Master Prompt
Open Composer (Cmd + I), make sure it is in Agent Mode, and paste this prompt to generate the foundation:

"I need to build a high-end personal portfolio with a 3D-feeling glassmorphism design.

AI Persona: Create a floating 'Bubble AI' assistant. It must use a bilingual LLM integration (like OpenAI or Gemini) to chat with visitors in Algerian Derja and English.

Derja Integration: Hardcode a set of common Derja responses for 'Salam', 'Wach rak', 'Chkoun nta' to ensure the vibe is authentic even before the API call.

Design: Use dark mode, glowing accents, and smooth Framer Motion transitions. Include sections for: Hero (with my AI persona avatar), Projects (hover-effect cards), and a 'Deep Work' log.

Autonomy: You are in God Mode. Look at the structure and automatically add features I might have missed (e.g., a 'Status' indicator for if I'm currently for hire, a dark/light mode toggle, and a visitor analytics dashboard).

Persona Personality: The AI should act as my digital twin. If asked in Derja, it must respond with the warmth of an Algerian dev. Please generate the full file structure and core components now."

Step 3: Implementing the "Bubble" Assistant
To get that specific "Bubble" behavior, use this follow-up prompt in the chat:

"Now, build the BubbleAssistant.tsx component. Use a floating-action-button (FAB) style. When opened, it shows a chat interface. Add a 'Derja-First' toggle. Ensure the AI uses a system prompt that says: 'You are the digital persona of a developer. Speak in a mix of Algerian Derja and English. Use Latin script (e.g., 'Wach rak sahbi') for Derja unless Arabic script is requested.'"

Key Features Suggested for "God Mode"
Since you want the AI to suggest things automatically, here is what I (as your AI collaborator) suggest adding to your codebase:

Time-Based Greetings: "Sbah l'khir" in the morning, "Msel khir" in the evening.

Interactive Terminal: A small command-line interface in the footer for "power users" to navigate your portfolio.

Project "Deep Dive": Instead of just images, have a "View Process" button that shows the "Deep Work" behind each project (sketches, logic flow).   my info is username ( MR ROBOT ) Name: SAMET SALIM
Age: 24 years old
Date of Birth: December 16, 2001
Marital Status: Single
Location: Alger / Tébessa, Algeria
Phone: +213 669 98 97 86
Email: salimsamet00@gmail.com
GitHub: github.com/Mrrobot-rx
Portfolio: [https://flo.fun/sxlCK1n/  ]
Education and Training
2021 — First Year Law License (Incomplete)
Larbi Tebessi University, Faculty of Law, Tébessa
Field: Law
2019 — Baccalauréat
Secondary School
Field: Foreign Languages
Note: Transitioned from Law to Technology through self-directed learning in AI and Data Science.
Work Experience
Administrative Secretary Assistant — Military Hospital Project
Location: Staoueli, Alger
Period: May 26, 2023 to January 18, 2024
Responsibilities:
Reception, classification, and organization of mail and correspondence
Digital and physical archive management and organization
Data entry and administrative database maintenance
Achievements:
Identified inefficiencies in manual data processing workflows
Self-learned automation techniques to streamline repetitive tasks
Developed foundational skills in data management and systematic organization
Digital Skills
Programming: Python (pandas, numpy) — Independent user
AI and Machine Learning: Natural Language Processing, Prompt Engineering, AI Automation — Independent user
Web Development: Streamlit, Web Applications — Independent user
Data Analysis: Text preprocessing, Data cleaning, Sentiment analysis — Independent user
Version Control: Git, GitHub — Independent user
Tools and Platforms: VS Code, Hugging Face, OpenAI API — Independent user
Language Skills
Arabic (Fusha): Native (C2) — Full professional proficiency in all skills
Algerian Darja: Native (C2) — Full professional proficiency in all skills
English: Independent user (B2) — Studied university courses in English
French: Threshold user (B1) — Basic communication and writing skills
Projects
Algerian Arabic Sentiment Analysis Tool — January 2025
Independent Project — Self-directed Learning
Description: Built an interactive web application analyzing emotions in Algerian Arabic dialect (Darja)
Technologies used: Python, Streamlit, NLP, keyword extraction algorithms
Live demo: [https://algerian-sentiment-ggnh57jracm8pq3dxyccvj.streamlit.app/  ]
Source code: github.com/Mrrobot-rx/algerian-sentiment
Key features:
Real-time sentiment classification (positive, negative, neutral)
Custom text preprocessing for Algerian dialect
Responsive web interface with animated results
85 percent accuracy on test dataset of 200 plus labeled comments
Impact: Demonstrates practical application of AI for low-resource languages
Personal Skills
Self-directed Learning: Acquired advanced AI and ML skills independently through online resources, documentation, and practical experimentation
Problem-solving: Identified workflow inefficiencies in hospital administration and developed automation-focused solutions
Cross-cultural Communication: Fluent navigation between Arabic, French, and English linguistic and cultural contexts
Resilience: Maintained productivity and continuous learning despite challenging personal and professional circumstances
Project Management: Led complete project lifecycle from concept to deployment for sentiment analysis tool
Adaptability: Rapidly transitioned from Law to Technology through intensive self-study
Additional Information
Motivation Statement:
Transitioning from administrative work in a military hospital to self-taught AI development, I have demonstrated the ability to identify problems, learn independently, and build practical solutions. My background in foreign languages combined with technical skills in NLP positions me uniquely to bridge linguistic and technological gaps. I seek to formalize these skills through graduate study in Data Science, with the goal of developing AI tools that serve underrepresented languages and communities.
Career Objective:
To leverage self-taught AI expertise and linguistic background in graduate-level Data Science studies, focusing on Natural Language Processing for low-resource languages, ultimately contributing to technological inclusion in Algeria and the MENA region

**Defining the Objective**

I'm currently focused on defining the project's core objective: building a high-end personal portfolio SPA with React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. The target audience is Samet Salim, so I'm tailoring the design to be appealing to an expert in the field.


**Developing the "Bubble AI"**

I've got a solid plan for the "Bubble AI" assistant. It's envisioned as a floating chatbot offering bilingual support (Algerian Derja and English) with a unique personality – "Ya kho" and "Sahbi" are key. I'm prioritizing futuristic minimalism in the design, with glassmorphism effects and smooth animations. I'm focusing on CSS transitions/keyframes and Tailwind for animations, or using `d3`/`recharts` for data visualization if needed, since I will only use "popular and existing libraries" in the final output.


**Refining the Content**

I'm now diving deep into the portfolio's content structure. Key sections like Hero, Projects, Deep Work, Skills, Experience, and About are taking shape. I'm excited about the "God Mode" features, terminal in the footer, and specific data incorporation. The Gemini integration is a top priority; I'm simulating this with the provided `geminiService.ts` to link with the real API for the chatbot.

I'm structuring the architecture and file organization. I have defined `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, `types.ts`, `constants.ts`, `services/geminiService.ts`, and component folders, including `Navbar.tsx` which will use sticky positioning. I also will define routing and navigation. I'm focusing on defining component details.


**Outlining Key Components**

I'm currently fleshing out the core components now. I'm focusing on the `Hero`, `BubbleAssistant`, `Projects`, `DeepWork`, `Terminal`, and `Experience` sections. The `BubbleAssistant` is particularly challenging, as I need to handle state, messages, Derja toggles, and local hardcoded responses. It's crucial for the terminal's interaction and parsing commands. I'm also planning the design with `backdrop-blur`, `bg-opacity`, and gradients.


**Integrating the Application**

I'm now integrating the application, focusing on `App.tsx` to link components. I've drafted system instructions and hardcoded Derja responses for the "Bubble AI," shaping its personality. The API key requirement is addressed; I'll use `process.env.API_KEY` for real Gemini calls. I'll use the provided `geminiService.ts` to simulate Gemini.


**Refining the Animation**

I've decided to stick with Tailwind and CSS for animation simplicity. Instead of `framer-motion`, I'll leverage `transition-all` with durations and CSS transforms for hover effects and dynamic scaling. This ensures cleaner implementation for direct code integration.



**Defining the Visual Style**

I'm now nailing down the visual style: slate-900 background with emerald-400 accents, echoing a "Mr. Robot" aesthetic. I'm leveraging glassmorphism with `bg-white/10`, `backdrop-blur-md`, and `border-white/20`. Sans-serif typography is chosen, with monospaced font for code snippets.

**Addressing Specific User Needs**

I'm incorporating specific requests, like an "Open to Work" status indicator (green dot). I'll also implement a time-based greeting using "Sbah l'khir" or "Msel khir" and am highlighting projects such as the "Algerian Arabic Sentiment Analysis."

**Validating and Refining Code**

I'm mentally validating core code aspects: ensuring `createRoot` usage, correct imports, and proper Gemini integration via `gemini-3-flash-preview`. I'm also confirming XML format validity. I will prioritize `lucide-react` icon usage; I'll use standard imports or fall back to SVG icons to handle dependency challenges.

entries.description: Static data and content for the portfolio
Service to interact with Google Gemini API
Card component for individual projects with Deep Work view
Hero section with avatar and greeting
Main application component with layout and sections

The Pivot

Born in Tébessa and based in Alger, I started my journey in Law before discovering my true passion for technology.

The transition wasn't easy. It required deep, self-directed learning—from mastering Python basics to building complex NLP models for Algerian Derja.

My background in foreign languages (Arabic, French, English) gives me a unique edge in NLP, allowing me to understand the linguistic nuances that code needs to capture.
